name: Combo Organ
explanation: A polyphonic 1960s transistor combo organ. A divide-down design: every key fires one master pitch that is divided into octaves and re-summed through three bright rocker tabs — 16' Bass, 8' Mid and a 4'+reed Bright — for the hollow, buzzy, slightly reedy combo tone. Keys speak instantly (fast attack/release, no slow envelope), a scanner-style Vibrato wobbles the whole keyboard, and a Tone tilt runs the sound from dark and woody to bright and cutting. Controls: Bass (16'), Mid (8'), Bright (4'+reed), Vibrato Depth, Vibrato Rate, Tone and Level. Cheerful, bright, and fully polyphonic so chords ring.
